events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Task: Identification: PPE selection (commercial project). Observed field condition: voltage glove class wrong or overdue. Work rough-complete. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. This is PPE selection defect: electrical protective gloves wrong voltage class or overdue recertification. Risk high/critical? Electrical shock hazard. Need component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, maybe worn/degraded? The condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. Also non-compliant. Worn/degraded maybe if overdue. Use those. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value 0. confidence high 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. findings: short defect/hazard. actions: immediate corrective. rationale. references: NFPA 70E, OSHA 1910 Subpart I. Need ensure JSON valid.

  "findings": [
    "Voltage-rated gloves do not match the incident energy / voltage class required for the energized work task.",
    "Gloves are past the required recertification / test date, making their dielectric integrity unverified.",
    "Improper or overdue electrical PPE creates direct exposure to electric shock and arc flash hazards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the wrong-class and overdue voltage gloves from service immediately.",
    "Select and issue voltage-rated gloves with the correct class and voltage rating for the work per NFPA 70E.",
    "Send rubber insulating gloves for required dielectric testing and recertification before reuse.",
    "Verify glove color-coded class labels and test-date stamps before returning to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 70E and OSHA 1910 Subpart I require electrical protective gloves to be the correct voltage class for the exposed energized conductors and to be periodically tested and inspected. Wrong-class or overdue gloves are defective, non-compliant PPE that fail to provide assured protection against electric shock.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E",
    "OSHA 1910 Subpart I"
  ]
}
